repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem
The refactor to use `lsp.Capability` left around some cruft and semi-broken configuration for debounce.

## Solution
Clean up now-unnecessary helper methods and simplify deprecated ones to pass through to the non-deprecated ones. `debounce` now defaults to 200 for all buffers but is overridable via the deprecated start() method, which continues to take the max value specified for any client attached to the buffer.

If we wish to expose changing the debounce in a non-deprecated way, we will need to consider a "configuration" function, or even a bespoke method to set the debounce time on the main metaclass (or provide options to override for a particular buffer). General configuration of specific LSP features is an as-of-yet unsolved problem.

comment_data.body:
Are we sure that this won't throw any errors? As `bufnr` and `client_id` are mutually exclusive in `enable` method.

https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/4ed844be59ff6ee7f061a0d58a21bf7bea27c3d8/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/_capability.lua#L118-L127
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -827,66 +800,24 @@ function M.start(bufnr, client_id, opts)
   vim.validate('bufnr', bufnr, 'number')
   vim.validate('client_id', client_id, 'number')
 
-  bufnr = vim._resolve_bufnr(bufnr)
-
-  opts = opts or {}
-  assert(
-    (not opts.debounce or type(opts.debounce) == 'number'),
-    'opts.debounce must be a number with the debounce time in milliseconds'
-  )
-
-  local client = vim.lsp.get_client_by_id(client_id)
-  if not client then
-    vim.notify('[LSP] No client with id ' .. client_id, vim.log.levels.ERROR)
-    return
-  end
-
-  if not vim.lsp.buf_is_attached(bufnr, client_id) then
-    vim.notify(
-      '[LSP] Client with id ' .. client_id .. ' not attached to buffer ' .. bufnr,
-      vim.log.levels.WARN
-    )
-    return
-  end
+  M.enable(true, { bufnr = bufnr, client_id = client_id })

comment_data.body: Yeah sure enough. I didn't look closely enough at this capability replacement function (enable vs the original start()) but I don't actually see any reason why the filter needs to be mutually exclusive. We can probably just remove this assert (and backport this, since this PR was backported and the deprecated function will now assert as you point out). @ofseed @justinmk

comment_data.body: i think it was just a defensive, i.e. i'm not sure we have filtering implemented for that cominbation. i think the door is open to changing that

comment_data.body: Yeah I looked at the implementation. Having both works fine, it doesn't need to be mutually exclusive. I think we can just drop the assert--it's not documented anywhere that the filter needs to be one or the other (or neither).

comment_data.body:
The reason for adding this assertion is that using two filters simultaneously creates ambiguity, which I've explained here: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37690. 

It serves as a defensive measure to prevent unexpected results in some complex use cases. However, if this ends up causing more confusion than it prevents, I am open to remove it.

comment_data.body:
Didn't even know that PR existed and I 100% agree with @gpanders and @mfussenegger on it. Naturally I expected putting both options in the filter to just work (and it's unambiguously AND), and since it wasn't documented I just assumed this is how it behaved. This will certainly bite other people using the enable command on capabilities.

We should probably merge that PR to remove the assertion, and backport it to un-break the deprecated function call here. My 2 cents at least.

comment_regular_data.body:
beautiful! I see -70 delta, I merge 🌈 

> General configuration of specific LSP features is an as-of-yet unsolved problem.

for debounce specifically, a client-global config seems perfectly fine, don't think we need feature-specific debounce config...

title: feat(lsp): clean up semantic token initialization and debounce
